name: NullBot

consumes.minimum: 1 active CompItem + at least 1 selected layer
consumes.description: Reads the active CompItem, iterates over comp.selectedLayers to gather each layer's position.value, threeDLayer flag, inPoint/outPoint, and index. Derives the union time span and topmost index for null placement.

functions: calculate average [x, y] position of all selected layers
create a null layer named 'NullBot' at the calculated average position
inherit 3D mode from selected layers (sets threeDLayer if any selected layer is 3D)
parent every selected layer to the new null
move the null before the topmost selected layer (moveBefore)
set null inPoint/outPoint to the combined time span of all selected layers
wrap all operations in an undo group named 'NullBot'

scriptSim.scenario: success — two selected AV layers, null created at average position
scriptSim.inputs: Active comp 'Main Comp' (1920×1080, 5 s, 30 fps) with two AV layers selected: Layer A at [300, 200] (in 0 s – out 3 s) and Layer B at [500, 400] (in 1 s – out 5 s)

notes: Script is fully headless — no ScriptUI dialog or panel is displayed.
Position averaging is 2D only; Z position of 3D layers is not factored into the average.
Negative-stretch (reversed) layers have inPoint/outPoint swapped before the union span calculation.
The null is named 'NullBot' (not 'Null Control' as noted in the @usage JSDoc — the source code takes precedence).
Null timing is clamped to the union span of selected layers, not the full comp duration.
If only one layer is selected the null is positioned at that layer's position exactly.
